title: Tilray Brands' Bullish Beverage Strategy: What's Behind the Optimism?
text: Tilray's beverage segment faced headwinds in Q2 fiscal 2026, with net revenues declining 10.6% year-over-year to $50.1 million. The company attributed this downturn to continued category challenges within the craft beer segment and competitive pressures. Through Project 420, Tilray has delivered $27 million in annualized cost savings and targets $33 million total. The company is optimizing its portfolio through SKU rationalization and margin-focused efforts. Recent retail product resets in spring 2025 improved distribution of core brands including Shock Top and SweetWater Brewing products. Several acquired beverage brands that previously declined are presently showing promising results with healthier growth trends, helping regain lost retail shelf space. The company operates over 45% of Canada's THC beverage market and remains positioned to enter the U.S. market if federal cannabis legalization occurs. International expansion represents another growth avenue. HiBall Energy will launch in the U.K. in Q4, with planned expansion into the Middle East and Africa. Tilray intends to leverage future strategic partnerships to expand its Beverage business into new markets worldwide, with a focus on craft beer and nonalcoholic drinks.
